items.title: Greenland's self-determination is non-negotiable, PM tells Trump envoy
items.summary_auto: Greenland's Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen said Monday that he had a respectful and positive meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump 's special envoy to the Arctic territory, but that he made it clear that the Greenlandic people continue to insist on self-determination.

items.title: Manitoba selling off shelved U.S. booze that's set to expire in fall
items.summary_auto: There weren't any lineups but there were mile-long smiles from Winnipeggers who took advantage of another sale of U.S. booze at Manitoba Liquor Marts on Monday — the first time the province has offered it up since December.

items.title: One American tests positive for Ebola virus amid outbreak in Congo and Uganda
items.summary_auto: The U.S. Centers for Disease Control and ‌Prevention confirmed on Monday that ​one American has tested ​positive for Ebola as part ​of their work in the Democratic ‌Republic of Congo.

items.title: California jury in Elon Musk lawsuit unanimously sides with OpenAI
items.summary_auto: A U.S. jury on Monday ruled against Elon Musk in his lawsuit against OpenAI, finding the artificial intelligence company not liable to the world's richest person for having ‌allegedly strayed from its original mission to benefit humanity.

items.title: Canada captures 3rd straight win at men’s hockey worlds with 5-1 victory over Denmark
items.summary_auto: Porter Martone had a goal and an assist, Sidney Crosby had four assists and Canada defeated Denmark 5-1 for its third straight win at the men's hockey world championship on Monday in Fribourg, Switzerland.

items.title: Ottawa Charge staying confident ahead of must-win Game 3 on home ice
items.summary_auto: The Walter Cup final is headed to the Canadian Tire Centre on Monday night as the Ottawa Charge battle the Montreal Victoire in a must-win Game 3.

items.title: World Gymnastics lifts all restrictions on Russian, Belarusian athletes
items.summary_auto: World Gymnastics has lifted all restrictions on Russian and Belarusian athletes, allowing them to return to international competitions ‌under their national flags with immediate effect, it said on Monday.

items.title: Thinking of renting out your home short-term for World Cup? Experts say the extra cash isn't always worth it
items.summary_auto: Short-term rentals should be considered a formal business, financial planner Kelly Ho said, not a casual arrangement. This is not the same as family staying overnight.

items.title: Challenges and missteps early in Congo's Ebola outbreak likely delayed detection, sources say
items.summary_auto: Burial rituals allowed the virus to spread before alarms were raised, while testing missteps delayed detection, according to sources. Experts say delays could hurt efforts to stem the spread of the deadly illness, which has been declared a public health emergency of international concern.

items.title: Documents reveal Uber's behind-the-scenes lobbying in Halifax
items.summary_auto: Halifax Mayor Andy Fillmore delayed a vote on stricter regulations just one day after meeting with an Uber lobbyist, and his approved motion for a review committee used language nearly identical to Uber's official statements.

items.title: Toronto police officers charged in Spain with ‘serious’ allegations
items.summary_auto: Three Toronto police officers were charged while on vacation in Barcelona, a Toronto Police Service spokesperson said. Toronto police did not provide details about the allegations, saying only that they are serious.

items.title: 3 off-duty Toronto police officers charged in Spain with ‘serious’ allegations
items.summary_auto: Three Toronto police officers were charged while on vacation in Barcelona, a Toronto Police Service spokesperson said. Toronto police did not provide details about the allegations, saying only that they are serious.

items.title: Renowned Cree cellist and composer Cris Derksen remembered as trailblazer, innovator
items.summary_auto: Cris Derksen, 45, died in a highway crash in northern Alberta. Reports from friends and family say Derksen was fatally injured Friday driving home after attending the funeral of their father in Tallcree First Nation, near Fort Vermilion, Alta.

items.title: Habs and Victoire playing on the same day turns Montreal into playoff hockey heaven
items.summary_auto: What is Montreal like when the Canadiens and the Victoire are both in town for pivotal playoff matchups? CBC's Melinda Dalton went to both games to capture the city's hockey passion.

items.title: The Rockies have a people problem. Here's one potential solution
items.summary_auto: A plan known as "destination stewardship" is aiming to improve managing human use in the Bow Valley. It would formalize communication between all levels of government and different agencies in the region.

items.title: Vancouver mansion owners sue neighbours over hedge removal
items.summary_auto: A lawyer who isn't involved in the case said he would prefer to see neighbours speak to each other over trees and hedges, rather than drag each other to court.

items.title: The survival of small town cinemas: Some Alberta screens go dark while others reinvent the reel
items.summary_auto: For more than a century, movies have remained a popular pastime across the country. But as technology evolves and economies change, how are small-time theatres keeping up? And what's at stake if another rural Alberta town loses its downtown anchor?

items.title: Alberta students being treated for rabies exposure after bat encounter on school bus
items.summary_auto: A school bus ride in Drumheller took an unexpected turn when a bat turned out to be on board. At least three students on the bus are being treated for rabies exposure after making direct contact with the bat last week, according to two parents.

items.title: Iranian Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi sent home after discharge from hospital
items.summary_auto: Iranian Nobel Peace Prize winner Narges Mohammadi has been discharged from a cardiac care unit and sent home, weeks after being transferred from prison to hospital following a suspected heart attack, a foundation run by her family said on Monday.

items.title: Doctors call for strong oversight as Alberta works toward public-private practice model
items.summary_auto: The Alberta Medical Association is calling on the provincial government to adopt dozens of safeguards as the province crafts regulations designed to allow physicians to practise in both the public and private systems.

items.title: Doctors call for strong oversight, workforce protections as Alberta works towards dual practice model
items.summary_auto: The Alberta Medical Association is calling on the provincial government to adopt dozens of safeguards as the province crafts regulations designed to allow physicians to practise in both the public and private systems.

items.title: Pop singer Shakira acquitted by Spanish court of tax fraud
items.summary_auto: A Spanish court acquitted Colombian singer Shakira in a tax fraud case, ordering the government to return some $87 million Cdn in fines and interest, according to a court document. Tax officials say they're appealing the decision and no payment will be made until there's a final ruling.

items.title: Canadians among activists detained on Gaza flotilla after Israeli interception
items.summary_auto: Israel's navy intercepted an activist flotilla in waters off Cyprus on Monday, halting the group's latest effort to challenge a blockade of Gaza.

items.title: How 'Cape Bretoner' became an ethnicity option on the census
items.summary_auto: Statistics Canada says 13,065 people reported an ethnic origin of “Cape Bretoner” on the last census, either alone or in combination with other ethnicities.

items.title: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship docks in the Netherlands for disinfection
items.summary_auto: The MV Hondius was still carrying 25 crew members and two medical personnel as it reached Europe's largest port on Monday morning, after its passengers disembarked on the Spanish island of Tenerife last week.

items.title: Is the U.S. military running out of weapons?
Girls and young women are more likely to tear their ACLs. Here’s why — and how to prevent it
items.summary_auto: Female athletes are more likely than their male counterparts to tear their anterior cruciate ligaments — and may be less likely to return to sport afterward. The injury is devastating, but strength and agility training can reduce the risk.

items.title: Jury set to deliberate in Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI
items.summary_auto: Elon Musk, the world's richest person, has accused OpenAI CEO Sam Altman of "stealing" a "charity" and is calling for his ouster from the company's leadership. A parade of wealthy Silicon Valley figures have taken the witness stand over the past several weeks, while protesters outside the courthouse took aim at both sides.

items.title: 4 crew members ejected safely after 2 U.S. navy jets crash during air show
items.summary_auto: The collision involved two U.S. Navy EA18-G Growlers from the Electronic Attack Squadron 129 in Whidbey Island, Washington, said Cmdr. Amelia Umayam, spokesperson for Naval Air Forces, U.S. Pacific Fleet.
